params.name: Apollo and Daphne
params.description:
On Feb 24, 1622 AD, Bernini began sculpting Apollo and Daphne.
This sculpture is one of the most representative works of the master sculptor Bernini. It is based on a mythical story: Apollo offended the god of love, who then ignited the flame of love in him. He pursued Daphne, the daughter of the river god, but was rejected. Unable to escape, Daphne transformed into a laurel tree, and the sculpture's shape is taken from this scene. The entire sculpture has a strong sense of movement, with the …
